heading: Effective date; special rules

notes: References in TextThis chapter, referred to in subsec. (e)(1), was in the original “this Act”, meaning , known as the Employee Retirement Income Security Act of 1974. Change of NameCommittee on Education and Labor of House of Representatives changed to Committee on Education and the Workforce of House of Representatives by House Resolution No. 5, One Hundred Eighteenth Congress, .Jan. 9, 2023Committee on Labor and Public Welfare of Senate abolished and replaced by Committee on Human Resources of Senate, effective . See Rule XXV of Standing Rules of Senate, as amended by Senate Resolution No. 4 (popularly cited as the “Committee System Reorganization Amendments of 1977”), approved . See, also, Rule XXV of Standing Rules of Senate adopted . L. 96–364, title IV, § 40594 Stat. 1303Sept. 26, 1980“(a)Except as otherwise provided in the amendments made by this Act [see Short Title of 1980 Amendment note set out under ] and in subsection (b), if the way in which any such amendment will apply to a particular circumstance is to be set forth in regulations, any reasonable action during the period before such regulations take effect shall be treated as complying with such regulations for such period.section 1001 of this title“(b)Subsection (a) shall not apply to any action which violates any instruction issued, or temporary rule prescribed, by the agency having jurisdiction but only if such instruction or rule was published, or furnished to the party taking the action, before such action was taken.”

children.label: a
children.text: The provisions of this subchapter take effect on .September 2, 1974
which is not a multiemployer plan,
which terminates after , and before ,June 30, 1974September 2, 1974
to which would apply if that section were effective beginning on , andsection 1321 of this titleJuly 1, 1974

with respect to which a notice is filed with the Secretary of Labor and received by him not later than 10 days after , except that, for reasonable cause shown, such notice may be filed with the Secretary of Labor and received by him not later than , stating that the plan is a plan described in paragraphs (1), (2), and (3).September 2, 1974October 31, 1974

Except as provided in paragraphs (2), (3), and (4), the corporation shall not pay benefits guaranteed under this subchapter with respect to a multiemployer plan which terminates before . Whenever the corporation exercises the authority granted under paragraph (2) or (3), the corporation shall notify the Committee on Education and Labor and the Committee on Ways and Means of the House of Representatives, and the Committee on Labor and Public Welfare and the Committee on Finance of the Senate.August 1, 1980

children.label: A
children.text: the plan was maintained during the 60 months immediately preceding the date on which the plan terminates, and

children.label: B
children.text: the corporation determines that the payment by the corporation of benefits guaranteed under this subchapter with respect to that plan will not jeopardize the payments the corporation anticipates it may be required to make in connection with benefits guaranteed under this subchapter with respect to multiemployer plans which terminate after .July 31, 1980

children.label: A
children.text: the plan has been in effect for at least 5 years,

children.label: B
children.text: the plan has been in substantial compliance with the funding requirements for a qualified plan with respect to the employees and former employees in those employment units on the basis of which the participating employers have contributed to the plan for the preceding 5 years, and

children.label: C
children.text: the participating employers and employee organization or organizations had no reasonable recourse other than termination.

children.label: A
children.text: may establish requirements for the continuation of payments which commenced before , with respect to retired participants under the plan,January 2, 1974

children.label: B
children.text: may not, notwithstanding any other provision of this subchapter, make payments with respect to any participant under such a plan who, on , was receiving payment of retirement benefits, in excess of the amounts and rates payable with respect to such participant on that date,January 1, 1974

children.label: C
children.text: shall review from time to time payments made under the authority granted to it by paragraphs (2) and (3), and reduce or terminate such payments to the extent necessary to avoid jeopardizing the ability of the corporation to make payments of benefits guaranteed under this subchapter in connection with multiemployer plans which terminate after , without increasing premium rates for such plans.July 31, 1980

children.label: d
children.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of this subchapter, guaranteed benefits payable by the corporation pursuant to its discretionary authority under this section shall continue to be paid at the level guaranteed under , without regard to any limitation on payment under subparagraph (C) of subsection (c)(4).section 1322 of this title
Except as provided in paragraphs (2), (3), and (4), the amendments to this chapter made by the Multiemployer Pension Plan Amendments Act of 1980 shall take effect on .September 26, 1980

children.label: A
children.text: Except as provided in this paragraph, part 1 of subtitle E, relating to withdrawal liability, takes effect on .September 26, 1980

children.label: B
children.text: For purposes of determining withdrawal liability under part 1 of subtitle E, an employer who has withdrawn from a plan shall be considered to have withdrawn from a multiemployer plan if, at the time of the withdrawal, the plan was a multiemployer plan as defined in as in effect at the time of the withdrawal.section 1301(a)(3) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: the date on which the last collective bargaining agreement providing for employer contributions under the plan, which was in effect on , expires, without regard to extensions agreed to on or after , orSeptember 26, 1980September 26, 1980

children.label: B
children.text: 3 years after .September 26, 1980
shall take effect on .Section 1415 of this titleSeptember 26, 1980

children.label: A
children.text: an employer has withdrawn from a multiemployer plan under , andsection 1363 of this title

children.label: B
children.text: the employer is liable to the corporation under such section,

In any case in which the plan is not so terminated within the period described in paragraph (1), the liability of the employer is abated and any payment held in escrow shall be refunded without interest to the employer or the employer’s bond shall be cancelled.

children.label: A
children.text: apply , as in effect before the amendments made by the Multiemployer Pension Plan Amendments Act of 1980, to such plan,section 1363(d) of this title

children.label: B
children.text: assess liability against the withdrawn employer with respect to the resulting terminated plan,

children.label: C
children.text: guarantee benefits under the terminated plan under , as in effect before such amendments, andsection 1322 of this title

children.label: D
children.text: if necessary, enforce such action through suit brought under .section 1303 of this title

The corporation shall use the revolving fund used by the corporation with respect to basic benefits guaranteed under in guaranteeing benefits under a terminated plan described in this subsection.section 1322a of this title

children.label: A
children.text: which was effective on , and which remained in effect through , andJanuary 12, 1979May 15, 1982

children.label: B
children.text: under which contributions to a multiemployer plan were to cease on ,January 12, 1982

children.label: i
children.text: had ceased all covered operations under a multiemployer plan before , and had relocated its operations to a new facility in another State, andJune 30, 1981

children.label: ii
children.text: had notified a local union representative on , that the employer had tentatively decided to discontinue operations and relocate to a new facility in another State, andMay 14, 1980

children.label: B
children.text: all State and local approvals with respect to construction of and commencement of operations at the new facility had been obtained, a contract for construction had been entered into, and construction of the new facility had begun before ,September 26, 1980

children.label: i
children.text: The preceding provisions of this section shall not apply with respect to amendments made to this subchapter in provisions enacted after .October 22, 1986
